name: Vegetable Stuffed Chayote
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Cups Whole Grain Bread Crumbs, Toasted
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the chayote lengthwise and place halves in enough cold salted water to cover.
2. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat. Cover and simmer for 12 to 15 minutes or until tender. Drain.
3. When cool enough to handle, remove seeds. Scoop out pulp to within 1/4 inch of the skin. Invert shells; set aside to drain. Chop pulp and drain. If necessary, squeeze excess liquid using paper towels. Set aside.
4. Meanwhile, for stuffing: Cook mushrooms, sweet pepper, onion, and garlic in hot margarine until tender but not brown. Remove from heat.
5. Stir in chayote pulp, toasted bread crumbs, 1/3 cup of Parmesan cheese, beaten egg, cilantro, salt, pepper, and dissolved bouillon granules into water. Mix well.
6. Spoon stuffing into the chayote shells.
7. Place shells in a 2-quart square baking dish. Cover and bake at 350 degrees for about 25 minutes or until heated through. Sprinkle with remaining Parmesan cheese. Bake for an additional 3 to 5 minutes until cheese melts. Makes 4 servings.
8. To toast bread crumbs, spread them in a single layer in a shallow baking pan. Bake at 350 degrees for about 8 minutes or until toasted.
